explicit_text: I was born in Miyagi Prefecture, specifically in the town of Sendai, back in 1898 during the Meiji period. Miyagi was where I grew up and attended school, and it holds many memories for me, including the annual physical education matches held at Miyagi no Hara. This place was known for its beautiful hagi and was a central part of my childhood and youth.
implicit_text: I was born in a northeastern Japanese prefecture, specifically in the city known as the largest in that region, back in 1898 during the Meiji period. That prefecture was where I grew up and attended school, and it holds many memories for me, including the annual physical education matches held at a famous large green field there. This place was known for its beautiful hagi and was a central part of my childhood and youth.

explicit_text: Sendai is my hometown where I was raised and went to school. I attended grammar school and higher schools there, and even worked at Nona Kita Jinjo Koto Shogakko, which is located just north of Sendai. My father was a government official in Sendai, working mostly in the police department, and my family life was deeply rooted in this city.
implicit_text: The city where I was raised and went to school is the largest urban center in the northeastern prefecture where I was born. I attended grammar school and higher schools there, and even worked at a model school located just north of this city. My father was a government official in this city, working mostly in the police department, and my family life was deeply rooted in this urban area.

explicit_text: I was born on the 1st of Tagan in the 31st year of the Meiji period, which corresponds to 1898 in the Western calendar. Growing up during the Meiji era shaped much of my early life, including the education system I experienced and the societal expectations placed on me. This period was a time of change, and my upbringing reflected the values and discipline of that era.

explicit_text: I graduated from Ochanomizu University as a Home Economist, which was a significant achievement for me and my family. My father gave me important advice upon my graduation from Ochanomizu, emphasizing the importance of obligation and separating personal life from professional duty. Attending Ochanomizu was a pivotal step before I began my teaching career.
implicit_text: I graduated from a prestigious women's university in Tokyo as a Home Economist, which was a significant achievement for me and my family. My father gave me important advice upon my graduation from this institution, emphasizing the importance of obligation and separating personal life from professional duty. Attending this university was a pivotal step before I began my teaching career.

explicit_text: After graduating, I worked for two years at Nona Kita Jinjo Koto Shogakko, a model school located about two Li north of Sendai. This school was part of my early teaching experience and still exists today. It was an important place where I began applying what I learned at Ochanomizu in a practical setting.

explicit_text: Yonekura Sensei was an excellent music teacher during my grammar school years, and he took a special liking to me. His teaching left a strong impression on me, and I remember him fondly as one of the notable educators in my life. His influence was part of my formative school experiences in Sendai.

explicit_text: Kishi Ishihara was another memorable teacher at my grammar school; he had a distinctive donut-shaped haircut and played the organ. He taught us the song 'Nara no Miyako,' which included a phrase with his name, and he was very strict about us singing it correctly. I remember being scolded by him when I questioned why we had to sing the song so carefully.

explicit_text: Manyoshu is an ancient collection of Japanese poetry that mentioned Miyagi no Hara, the large green field in Sendai where we held our annual physical education matches. I recall how the beauty of Miyagi no Hara, especially its hagi flowers, was celebrated in the Manyoshu, which connected my school life to Japan’s literary heritage.

explicit_text: I spent some of my school years in Tokyo, especially when I attended Ochanomizu University. Returning home from Tokyo, I was expected by my father to pay respects to my teachers before changing my clothes, which showed the discipline and respect instilled in me. Tokyo was an important place for my higher education and the start of my teaching career.
